title: 9 Best Herbal Teas For Numbness In Hands
intro: Herbal teas for numbness in hands are a natural remedy used to alleviate numbness and tingling sensations in the hands. This condition, often caused by nerve damage or compression, can be treated with herbal teas due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce swelling and promote blood flow to the affected area. Some popular herbal teas used to treat numbness in hands include ginger tea, which helps to reduce inflammation and promote circulation, peppermint tea, which aids in relaxation and reduces numbness, and turmeric tea, which contains curcumin that has anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, other herbal teas such as rosemary tea, which improves circulation and reduces numbness, ginseng tea, which helps to increase blood flow and reduce fatigue, and Echinacea tea, which has antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation, can also be used to treat numbness in hands.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat numbness in hands, also known as peripheral neuropathy, due to their vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the nerves, thereby alleviating numbness and tingling sensations in the hands. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba tea, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide, help to increase blood flow and reduce oxidative stress, which contribute to the relief of numbness in the hands. The benefits of using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat numbness in hands include improved circulation, reduced inflammation, and enhanced nerve function, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Place the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour the boiling water over the leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally after meals, for numbness in hands.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to treat numbness in hands, but possible side effects may include dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its blood-thinning properties, as well as allergic reactions in some individuals. To avoid adverse effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase it, be cautious when consuming ginkgo biloba with other medications, and monitor blood pressure, as excessive consumption can lead to increased risk of bleeding.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor about using Ginkgo biloba tea for numbness in hands
Start with 1/2 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea 2 times a day
Drink tea 30 minutes before meals for better absorption
Increase to 1 cup of tea 3 times a day if numbness persists
Monitor numbness and adjust dosage as advised by doctor

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, made from the rhizomes of the ginger plant, have been traditionally used to treat numbness in the hands, a condition known as paresthesia. The anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation, particularly the presence of gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce pain and inflammation in the affected areas, thereby alleviating numbness. The bioactive constituents of Zingiber officinale teas, including 6-gingerol and 6-shogaol, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, leading to a decrease in pain and numbness. The benefits of using Zingiber officinale teas to treat numbness in the hands include reduced pain and discomfort, improved circulation, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 tablespoon of dried Zingiber officinale root, 1 cup of water, and a strainer.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried Zingiber officinale root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid using a strainer.
Drink the Zingiber officinale tea when it's warm or at room temperature to help relieve numbness in hands.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat numbness in hands, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and dizziness in some individuals, especially when consumed in large quantities. Precautions to take when using Zingiber officinale teas include starting with small doses, avoiding consumption before surgery or with anticoagulant medications, and being cautious in people with bleeding disorders or stomach ulcers.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Zingiber officinale tea for numbness in hands.
Drink 1/2 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried ginger per 1 cup of water for 3 days.
Gradually increase the ginger to 2 teaspoons per 1 cup of water if needed.
Continue using Zingiber officinale tea for 7-10 days to see noticeable results.

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas have been used to treat numbness in hands, a condition often associated with Raynaud's disease or peripheral neuropathy, due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by improving blood circulation, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, and promoting nerve regeneration. The bioactive constituents of Achillea millefolium teas, including sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, contribute to their therapeutic effects by scavenging free radicals, inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes, and modulating the immune response. The benefits of using Achillea millefolium teas to treat numbness in hands include improved circulation, reduced pain and discomfort, and enhanced overall nerve function, making it a promising natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Achillea millefolium leaves. You can find them at a health food store or online.
Place 1 tablespoon of dried leaves in a tea infuser or a small piece of cheesecloth.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Remove the pot from the heat and add the tea infuser or cheesecloth with the dried leaves. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves. Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help with numbness in hands.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can be used as a potential remedy for numbness in the hands, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, interactions with other medications, and an increased risk of bleeding due to its antiplatelet properties. To use Achillea millefolium teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose, monitor for any adverse reactions, avoid taking it with other blood thinners or medications that slow blood clotting, and be cautious when consuming it if you have a history of bleeding disorders or are prone to bruising.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Achillea millefolium tea 3 times a day.
Start with 1 tablespoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water.
Reduce numbness by massaging hands with warm oil after tea consumption.
Use Achillea millefolium tea for 2-3 weeks to notice noticeable improvements.
Monitor and adjust the dosage to 2 tablespoons per cup if numbness persists.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, derived from the fragrant herb rosemary, have been traditionally used to treat numbness in hands, also known as peripheral neuropathy. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including carnosic acid, rosmarinic acid, and camphor, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to alleviate numbness by improving blood circulation and reducing oxidative stress. By promoting the regeneration of nerve cells and improving the function of the nervous system, Rosmarinus officinalis teas provide relief from numbness, tingling, and pain in the hands, ultimately restoring sensation and motor function. The benefits of using Rosmarinus officinalis teas for this ailment include reduced symptoms, improved quality of life, and a natural alternative to pharmaceutical treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1 cup, 2-3 times a day to help with numbness in hands.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be an effective treatment for numbness in hands, but using them may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high concentration of volatile oils. To minimize the risk of side effects, individuals should start with small amounts, drink the tea in moderation, and avoid consuming it before bedtime as it can interfere with sleep patterns.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Rosmarinus officinalis tea 2 times a day.
Take 1 tablespoon of dried Rosmarinus officinalis in tea.
Use tea for 2 weeks to relieve numbness in hands.
Adjust dosage to 2 cups a day if numbness persists.
Combine tea with regular exercise for faster relief.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas, also known as English lavender tea, have been traditionally used to treat numbness in hands, a condition often associated with carpal tunnel syndrome. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain, promoting blood flow and nerve function. The bioactive constituents of Lavandula angustifolia, including linalool and linalyl acetate, possess analgesic and antispasmodic properties that help to relax muscles, reduce numbness, and improve nerve conduction. Regular consumption of Lavandula angustifolia teas may provide relief from numbness in hands, as well as promote overall well-being and reduce stress levels.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia leaves or 1 tablespoon of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it boils.
Add the Lavandula angustifolia leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help with numbness in hands.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat numbness in hands, but it may cause side effects such as drowsiness, headache, and stomach upset in some individuals due to its sedative properties. To use Lavandula angustifolia teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase it, as excessive consumption may lead to allergic reactions, interact with medications, or exacerbate underlying health conditions.
preparation_usage: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Lavandula angustifolia in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve numbness in hands.
Increase dosage to 2 cups a day if symptoms persist after 1 week of regular use.
Use Lavandula angustifolia tea for 2-3 weeks to see noticeable improvement in numbness symptoms.
Combine Lavandula angustifolia tea with other natural remedies under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

preparations.herb_desc: Mentha x piperita teas, also known as peppermint tea, have been traditionally used to treat numbness in hands due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents present in peppermint tea, including menthone, menthol, and limonene, help to reduce swelling and pain in the hands, thereby alleviating numbness. By reducing inflammation and promoting blood flow, peppermint tea helps to treat numbness in hands by increasing oxygenation and nutrient supply to the affected area. The benefits of using peppermint tea to treat numbness in hands include its non-invasive nature, ease of preparation, and lack of side effects, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.

Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for 1 minute.
Place the tea infuser with peppermint leaves into the cooled water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser.
Drink the tea and repeat as needed for numbness relief in hands.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Mentha x piperita teas can be used to treat numbness in hands, but its use may be accompanied by side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Mentha x piperita teas to treat this ailment, it is essential to take precautions, such as monitoring your body's response, starting with small doses, and avoiding consumption if you have a history of stomach problems or allergies.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor to confirm numbness diagnosis and Mentha x piperita tea usage.
Take 1 teaspoon of dried Mentha x piperita in a cup of hot water.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for 1 week to assess its effect.
Adjust dosage to 1/2 teaspoon if numbness persists after 1 week.
Monitor numbness and adjust tea usage as advised by your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas, also known as valerian root tea, have been traditionally used to treat numbness in hands, a condition often caused by nerve damage or compression. The herbal preparation's sedative, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties help to reduce nerve inflammation, promote relaxation, and improve blood circulation, all of which contribute to alleviating numbness in the hands. By consuming valerian root tea, individuals may experience improved nerve function, reduced numbness, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a potential natural remedy for this condition.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Valeriana officinalis teas can cause drowsiness, stomach upset, and headaches in some individuals when used to treat numbness in hands, and it is also known to interact with certain medications such as sedatives and antidepressants. To use Valeriana officinalis teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase it as needed, avoid taking it before driving or operating heavy machinery, and be cautious when consuming it with other herbal supplements.

preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Thymus vulgaris teas for numbness in hands may not provide relief as the study found that Thymus vulgaris (thyme) produced a contracture without a change in the twitch response, indicating no alteration in the nerve stimulation response.

The herbal preparation contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce swelling and promote blood flow to the affected areas, thereby alleviating numbness and pain. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea, including alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, play a crucial role in its therapeutic effects, as they have been shown to exhibit analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties. By using Echinacea purpurea teas, individuals can experience benefits such as reduced pain and numbness, improved circulation, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a potential natural remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Echinacea purpurea flowers.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the flowers.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into a cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for numbness in hands.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to potentially alleviate numbness in hands, but they may also cause side effects such as stomach upset, headaches, and allergic reactions in some individuals. It is essential to be cautious when consuming Echinacea purpurea teas to treat numbness in hands, as excessive consumption can lead to interactions with medications and exacerbate underlying health conditions, and individuals should be aware of their body's sensitivity to the herbal preparation.

preparations.herb_desc: Piper nigrum teas, derived from the black pepper plant, have been traditionally used to treat numbness in hands, a condition often associated with neuropathy. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain, promoting blood flow and nerve regeneration. The bioactive constituents of Piper nigrum, including piperine, piperic acid, and beta-carboline alkaloids, have been shown to exhibit neuroprotective and analgesic effects, which contribute to its therapeutic benefits. By promoting nerve function and reducing inflammation, Piper nigrum teas can help to alleviate numbness in hands, improving overall quality of life for individuals suffering from this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of dried Piper nigrum (black pepper) in a saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat until it starts to boil.
Add the dried Piper nigrum to the boiling water and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the Piper nigrum. Pour the tea into a cup.
Drink 1/2 cup of the Piper nigrum tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve numbness in hands.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Frontiers in pharmacology, Piper nigrum teas for numbness in hands have not been specifically mentioned in the study, as the study focuses on the neuroprotective potential of various Indian spices in age-related neurological disorders.
preparations.preparation_side_effect:
Piper nigrum teas can be used as a herbal remedy to alleviate numbness in hands, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea and stomach upset, as well as allergic reactions in some individuals.

Precautions when using Piper nigrum teas to treat this ailment include starting with a small dose to assess tolerance, avoiding consumption if you have a history of stomach problems or allergies, and not mixing it with other medications or herbal supplements.

preparations.herb_desc: Cassia auriculata teas have been traditionally used to treat numbness in hands, a condition often associated with neurological disorders. The analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to alleviate pain and reduce swelling in the affected area, thereby providing relief from numbness. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, have been found to have neuroprotective effects, which help to repair and regenerate damaged nerve cells, promoting nerve function and restoring sensation. The benefits of Cassia auriculata teas in treating numbness in hands include improved nerve conductivity, reduced muscle weakness, and enhanced overall neurological function, making it a promising natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Cassia auriculata leaves and flowers, or 2 teaspoons of dried leaves and flowers.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Cassia auriculata leaves and flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes, then let it steep for another 5 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day to help with numbness in hands.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cassia auriculata teas can be used to treat numbness in hands, but possible side effects include allergic reactions, such as skin rashes or itching, and gastrointestinal issues like nausea or stomach discomfort. Precautions to take when using Cassia auriculata teas include avoiding excessive consumption, as it may cause stomach upset, and monitoring blood sugar levels in individuals with diabetes, as it may interact with medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle tea, have been traditionally used to treat numbness in hands due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including silymarin and flavonoids, help to improve blood circulation and reduce oxidative stress, thereby alleviating numbness in the hands. The benefits of this herbal preparation include reducing inflammation and promoting the repair of nerve damage, which can contribute to the numbness in hands. Regular consumption of Silybum marianum teas has been found to be beneficial in managing numbness in hands, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Silybum marianum leaves and flowers. You can find them in health food stores or online.
Rinse the Silybum marianum leaves and flowers with cold water to clean them.
Combine 1 cup of fresh Silybum marianum leaves and flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Drink 1/2 cup of the Silybum marianum tea 2-3 times a day for numbness relief in your hands.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can help alleviate numbness in hands, but they may also cause side effects such as gastrointestinal issues like diarrhea and nausea due to its potential interaction with medications or exacerbating certain health conditions. Precautions to take when using Silybum marianum teas include monitoring blood sugar levels, as it may affect blood glucose control, and being cautious when consuming it with other medications, especially those for diabetes or blood thinners.
